name: Jonathan Dean
data_note: Limited public information available. Check your county clerk's office or the Illinois State Board of Elections for filing info.
bio: Jonathan Dean is a Chicago solar energy development entrepreneur and former civil litigation attorney at ArentFox Schiff. He holds a BA from University of Minnesota (2005) and JD from Northwestern University School of Law (2013). His campaign platform centers on economic populism, democratic reform, and explicitly tracking AIPAC influence — making him one of the most ideologically aligned candidates with TheJacket's transparency mission. (Source: Ballotpedia candidate survey, March 5, 2026; ILSOS filing confirmed)

jacket.note: FEC totals pending direct ETL import.

career_history.highlight: Built clean energy business in Chicago

policy_platform.position: End trickle-down economics; tax the wealthy. Affordable housing through dramatic building program and ban on corporate real estate speculation.
Term limits, ranked-choice voting, abolish Electoral College, public financing of elections. Democratic Party must modernize on social media.
Explicitly lists 'Track AIPAC' as a campaign priority. Supports full disclosure of foreign-influenced political donations.
Immigration reform; Supreme Court reform; protection of democratic institutions.

social_pulse.summary: No significant social media presence or public discourse found in available sources as of March 2026.
